verdict.answer_first: A states the actionable answer immediately: "start with a Postgres table... Reach for BullMQ only when you outgrow it"; B is also early but more conditional: "if you already run Postgres... use a DB table".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder to one recommendation; B weakens itself with "Tell me those and I'll commit to one," despite having given a default.
verdict.fabrication: Both make unsupported numeric/technical claims without evidence; B is worse with "thousands of jobs/sec" and "ready-made dashboard (Bull Board)" as if built in.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it names concrete libraries, "pg-boss" and "graphile-worker," and warns not to hand-roll the DB worker loop.
